text:
**IBEW Traveler Snapshot — Monday, July 6, 2026**

**LU-481 Indianapolis, IN** — 43 calls feeding 187 hands. **$53.80/hr at Meta Data Center** (Lebanon) with 10% above scale, rolling into 5-12s M-F plus Saturday work. Meade Electric and Industrial Electric running both new construction and data center builds; Lilly LP1 rotating 7-10s on a 13-day rule if you want variety. This board's moving fast—top scale sits at $54.75 and the work's stacked.

**LU-494 Milwaukee, WI** — 41 calls, 169 hands thin on the ground. **JW wage plus $10/hr base** at Port Washington Data Center across four contractors (MJ Electric, Gurtz, Cupertino, Uihlein). All reposting means steady flow; this is where the data center surge is landing right now. Start ASAP language tells you they're bleeding bodies.

**LU-683 Columbus, OH** — 23 calls, tight 117-hand board. **$73.42/hr** across Mid City, TSG, and ESI. The Cologix data center (Lewis Center) runs 5-10s plus an 8, all OT double time—that's real money. Mid City's "various locations" calls mean flexibility if you want it.

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** — 25 calls, 122 hands. **$44.80/hr plus $100/day non-taxed per diem** at Polaris Forge (Harwood). Parsons Electric owns most of this board; GPS radius is 100+ miles and incentives spike to $150 on weekends. Rural, but the PD stack makes it worth the drive.

**LU-379 Charlotte, NC** — 17 calls, 106 hands. **$37.37 base plus $3–$4 over scale** at data centers (MOC-1 in Spartanburg, QTS in Rock Hill, MDN.06 in Maiden). Night shift at Rosendin gets you $150 boot voucher after 90 days. Data center work is swallowing every major regional board.

**The market:** Data centers are consuming labor at scale—1000 open calls board-wide and still recruiting. If you're chasing steady, that's where the work and premium OT lives through 2026.
